_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-28 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD) (Revised 1/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-28: LVAD — MDHHS Approved 1/27/2023
purpose: Govern prehospital management of patients with Left Ventricular Assist Devices (LVADs). LVADs produce non-pulsatile flow — standard vital sign assessment methods may be unreliable. Critical safety rules: DO NOT use AED or mechanical CPR devices on LVAD patients. CPR compressions are LAST RESORT only.

sections.title: LVAD Overview and Vital Sign Assessment
points: LVAD creates continuous, non-pulsatile blood flow — patients may have NO palpable pulse and NO detectable blood pressure by standard methods even when the device is functioning
Assess perfusion by: skin color and temperature; capillary refill; mental status and level of consciousness
If the LVAD is functioning: patient will typically have normal skin color, intact mentation, and capillary refill < 2 seconds
Standard SpO2 and blood pressure cuff readings may be unreliable or unobtainable
LVAD driveline exits the upper abdomen — DO NOT cut, pull, remove, or damage the driveline or any connections under any circumstances
ALL LVAD connections are secured with locking devices — do not attempt to disconnect

sections.title: Critical Safety Rules
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-28: LVAD — Critical Safety Rules
points: DO NOT USE AED on LVAD patients — electrical shock to an LVAD patient via AED is PROHIBITED
DO NOT USE mechanical CPR compression devices (LUCAS, AutoPulse, etc.) on LVAD patients
CPR CHEST COMPRESSIONS are LAST RESORT ONLY — consult Medical Control IMMEDIATELY before initiating compressions

LVAD patients may have a BASELINE ventricular fibrillation or ventricular tachycardia — V-fib or V-tach on the monitor does NOT necessarily mean the patient is in cardiac arrest if the LVAD is functioning and the patient has signs of perfusion
ALL LVAD patients are receiving ANTICOAGULATION therapy — handle carefully; bleeding risk is elevated

sections.title: Allowed Electrical Interventions
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-28: LVAD — Allowed Electrical Therapy
points: Manual defibrillation is ALLOWED and may be used if the patient is in hemodynamically significant V-fib or V-tach without adequate perfusion
These interventions do NOT require disconnection of the LVAD

sections.title: Transport
points: Transport to an MCA-approved LVAD center
Patient should have a 'go bag' containing: charged spare batteries, backup controller, and power supply unit
Contact the LVAD center prior to or during transport if possible
Notify receiving hospital of LVAD presence and any device alarms on scene

deterioration_flags: LVAD alarming — note alarm type; contact LVAD center or Medical Control; transport immediately
Signs of poor perfusion despite LVAD running — contact Medical Control; CPR compressions LAST RESORT only
V-fib or V-tach on monitor with LVAD patient — assess perfusion signs FIRST (color, mentation, cap refill); if adequate perfusion = LVAD may be maintaining circulation; contact MC before intervention
LVAD driveline damaged or disconnected — do NOT attempt to reconnect; transport immediately; contact Medical Control and LVAD center
scope_notes: LVAD creates non-pulsatile flow — may have no pulse and no BP by standard methods; assess perfusion by skin, mental status, cap refill
DO NOT use AED on LVAD patients
DO NOT use mechanical CPR devices on LVAD patients
CPR compressions: LAST RESORT ONLY; consult MC immediately
Manual defibrillation, cardioversion, and TCP are ALLOWED
V-fib/V-tach may be BASELINE in LVAD patients — assess perfusion before intervening
All LVAD patients are anticoagulated — elevated bleeding risk
Driveline: DO NOT cut, pull, or damage
